handedness.Description: handedness of the participant as reported by the participant

polx_general.Description: general left-right political orientation of participant

polx_social.Description: left-right political orientation of participant on social issues

polx_econ.Description: left-right political orientation of participant on economic issues

ideology_parties.Description: which Dutch party would the participant vote for

ideology_parties_18_TEXT.Description: if no party selected, which party would the participant vote for

libertarianism.Description: How much value do you place on individual liberty and autonomy, as opposed to government regulation?

environmentalism.Description: To what extent do you see yourself as someone who cares about the environment and considers being environmentally responsible an important part of who you are?

pol_interest.Description: How interested are you in politics?

mfq_rel_care_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether or not someone suffered emotionally

mfq_rel_fairness_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether some people were treated differently than others

mfq_rel_loyalty_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether somones actions showed loved for his or her country

mfq_rel_authority_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone has shown too little respect for authority

mfq_rel_sanctity_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone has violated standards of purity and decency

mfq_rel_control_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone was good at math

mfq_rel_care_2.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone cared for someone weak or vulnerable

mfq_rel_fairness_2.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone has acted dishonestly

mfq_rel_loyalty_2.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone has betrayed his or her group

mfq_rel_authority_2.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone conformed to the traditions of society

mfq_rel_sanctity_2.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone did something disgusting

mfq_rel_care_3.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone was cruel

mfq_rel_fairness_3.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone's rights have been denied

mfq_rel_loyalty_3.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone has shown too little loyalty

mfq_rel_authority_3.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone's actions caused chaos or disorder

mfq_rel_sanctity_3.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Whether someone was behaving in a way that God would approve of

mfq_judg_care_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Compassion for those who suffer is the most important virtue

mfq_judg_fairness_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): When government makes laws, ensuring that everyone is treated fairly should be the most imporant principle

mfq_judg_loyalty_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): I am proud of my country's history

mfq_judg_authority_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Respect for authority is something all children need to learn

mfq_judg_sanctity_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): People should not do disgusting things, even if noone is harmed

mfq_judg_control_1.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): It is better to do something good than something bad

mfq_judg_care_2.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): One of the worst things a person could do is hurt a defenseless animal

mfq_judg_fairness_2.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Justice is the most important need for a society

mfq_judg_loyalty_2.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): People should be loyal to their relatives even when they have done something bad

mfq_judg_authority_2.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Men and women each have different roles in society

mfq_judg_sanctity_2.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): I think some actions are bad because they are unnatural

mfq_judg_care_3.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): It can never be right to kill a human being

mfq_judg_fairness_3.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): I think it is morally wrong that rich children inherit a lot of money while poor children inherit nothing

mfq_judg_loyalty_3.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): It is more important to be a team player than to express yourself

mfq_judg_authority_3.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): If I were a soldier and I disagreed with my superior's orders, I would still obey because it is my duty

mfq_judg_sanctity_3.Description: MFQ-30 (van Leuwen & Spiering, 2010): Chastisity is an important and valuable virtue

sdo_1.Description: SDO (Ho et al., 2015): An ideal society requires some groups to be on top and others to be on the bottom.

sdo_2.Description: SDO (Ho et al., 2015): Some groups of people are simply inferior to other groups.

sdo_3.Description: SDO (Ho et al., 2015): No one group should dominate in society.*

sdo_4.Description: SDO (Ho et al., 2015): Groups at the bottom are just as deserving as groups at the top.*

sdo_5.Description: SDO (Ho et al., 2015): Group equality should not be our primary goal.

sdo_6.Description: SDO (Ho et al., 2015): It is unjust to try to make groups equal.

sdo_7.Description: SDO (Ho et al., 2015): We should do what we can to equalize conditions for different groups.*

sdo_8.Description: SDO (Ho et al., 2015): We should work to give all groups an equal chance to succeed.*

rwa_1.Description: RWA (Altemeyer, 2022): Our country desperately needs a mighty leader who will do what has to be done to destroy the radical new ways and sinfulness that are ruining us.

rwa_2.Description: RWA (Altemeyer, 2022): Gays and lesbians are just as healthy and moral as anybody else.*

rwa_3.Description: RWA (Altemeyer, 2022): The “old-fashioned ways” and the “old-fashioned values” still show the best way to live.

rwa_5.Description: RWA (Altemeyer, 2022): Atheists and others who have rebelled against the established religions are no doubt every bit as good and virtuous as those who attend church regularly.*

rwa_6.Description: RWA (Altemeyer, 2022): God’s laws about abortion, pornography and marriage must be strictly followed before it is too late, and those who break them must be strongly punished.

rwa_7.Description: RWA (Altemeyer, 2022): What our country really needs is a strong, determined leader who will crush evil and take us back to our true path.

rwa_8.Description: RWA (Altemeyer, 2022): You have to admire those who challenged the law and the majority’s view by protesting for women’s abortion rights, for animal rights, or to abolish school prayer.*

rwa_9.Description: RWA (Altemeyer, 2022): Homosexuals and feminists should be praised for being brave enough to defy “traditional family values.”*

rwa_10.Description: RWA (Altemeyer, 2022): The only way our country can get through the crisis ahead is to get back to our traditional values, put some tough leaders in power, and silence the troublemakers spreading bad ideas.

rwa_11.Description: RWA (Altemeyer, 2022): Everyone should have their own lifestyle, religious beliefs, and sexual preferences, even if it makes them different from everyone else.*

IUS12_1.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): Unforeseen events upset me greatly.
IUS12_1.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

IUS12_2.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): It frustrates me not having all the information I need.
IUS12_2.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

IUS12_3.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): One should always look ahead so as to avoid surprises.
IUS12_3.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

IUS12_4.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): A small, unforeseen event can spoil everything, even with the best of planning.
IUS12_4.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

IUS12_5.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): I always want to know what the future has in store for me.
IUS12_5.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

IUS12_7.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): I can’t stand being taken by surprise.
IUS12_7.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

IUS12_8.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): I should be able to organize everything in advance.
IUS12_8.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

IUS12_9.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): Uncertainty keeps me from living a full life.
IUS12_9.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

IUS12_10.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): When it's time to act, uncertainty paralyses me.
IUS12_10.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

IUS12_11.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): When I am uncertain I can’t function very well.
IUS12_11.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

IUS12_12.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): The smallest doubt can stop me from acting.
IUS12_12.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

IUS12_13.Description: IUS (Caleton et al., 2008): I must get away from all uncertain situations.
IUS12_13.Units: 1 (not at all characteristic of me) to 5 (entirely characteristic of me)

dws_1.Description: Dangeous worldview: My knowledge and experience tells me that the social world we live in is basically a safe, stable and secure place in which most people are fundamentally good*

dws_2.Description: It seems that every year there are fewer and fewer truly respectable people, and more and more persons with no morals at all who threaten everyone else

dws_3.Description: Although it may appear that things are constantly getting more dangerous and chaotic, it really isn’t so. Every era has its problems, and a person’s chances of living asafe, untroubled life are better today than ever before*

dws_4.Description: Any day now chaos and anarchy could erupt around us. All the signs are pointing to it

dws_5.Description: There are many dangerous people in our society who will attack someone out of pure meanness, for no reason at all

dws_6.Description: The ‘‘end’’ is not near. People who think that earthquakes, wars, and famines mean God might be about to destroy the world are being foolish*

dws_7.Description: My knowledge and experience tells me that the social world we live in is basically a dangerous and unpredictable place, in which good, decent and moral people’s values and way of life are threatened and disrupted by bad people

dws_8.Description: Despite what one hears about ‘‘crime in the street,’’ there probably isn’t any more now than there ever has been*

dws_9.Description: If a person takes a few sensible precautions, nothing bad is likely to happen to him or her; we do not live in a dangerous world*

dws_10.Description: Every day as society become more lawless and bestial, a person’s chances of being robbed, assaulted, and even murdered go up and up

attention_check_1.Description: participant had to select a particular option from the scale
attention_check_1.Unit: True if passed; False if failed

attention_check_2.Description: participant had to select a particular option from the scale
attention_check_2.Unit: True if passed; False if failed

attention_self_report.Description: how much attention participant paid during survey

mfq_care_avg.Description: mean score for mfq care items

mfq_fairness_avg.Description: mean score for mfq fairness items

mfq_loyalty_avg.Description: mean score for mfq loyalty items

mfq_authority_avg.Description: mean score for mfq authority items

mfq_sanctity_avg.Description: mean score for mfq purity items
